crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Flexible workspace capacity to grow 16-18% this fiscal and next
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: Indian flexible workspace (flex) segment is set to increase its capacity by 16-18% over this and next fiscals to 140-145 million square feet (msf) - after an expansion with ~23% CAGR over the past three fiscals though fiscal 2026 - driven by rising demand from small and mid-sized global capability c

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: For banks, ECL norms to have one-time net impact of up to 120 bps
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: The Reserve Bank of India’s (RBI) final directions1 entailing, inter alia, transition to an expected credit loss (ECL) regime for domestic banks, would lead to a one-time net impact of up to 120 basis points (bps) on their Common Equity Tier 1 (CET-1) ratio.

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: IBC (Amendment) Act, 2026, signals intent to expedite resolutions
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: The 7th Amendment to the IBC  Act passed in April 2026, signals a clear intent towards minimising procedural delays, that have led to highly extended resolution timelines resulting in economic value erosion of assets.

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: CV volume driving to record, but growth to slow at 5-6% this fiscal
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: India’s commercial vehicle (CV) industry is expected to clock a record overall volume of ~12.4 lakh units in fiscal 2027, surpassing the previous peak of fiscal 2019. The industry had a strong outing in fiscal 2026, with a 13% rebound. Coming on that high base, growth is expected to moderate to 5–6%

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Pain in the pack to tear margin of alcobev makers 150-200 bps
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: Alcoholic beverage (alcobev) manufacturers in India are set to see their earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortisation (Ebitda) margin decline by 150-200 basis points (bps) this fiscal due to a rise in packaging costs following supply-chain disruptions caused by the ongoing West Asi

crisil-research.title: Crisil freight index dips in April
crisil-research.description: Freight rates across key trucking corridors1 softened in April 2026, largely due to a moderation in industrial and manufacturing activity following the year-end dispatch momentum witnessed in March 2026.

crisil-research.title: Shock and woe
crisil-research.description: We now expect India’s gross domestic product (GDP) to grow 6.6% and retail inflation to average 5.1% in fiscal 2027

crisil-research.title: Data Management as a Service (DMaaS)
crisil-research.description: A global asset management firm operating across a complex ecosystem of multiple data vendors and downstream systems faced significant challenges in establishing a consistent and reliable view of its entities and associated data.

crisil-research.title: RRR: Roti Rice Rate
crisil-research.description: The average cost of preparing a thali at home is calculated based on input prices prevailing in north, south, east and west India. The monthly change reflects the impact on the common man’s expenditure. The data also reveals the ingredients (cereals, pulses, broilers, vegetables, spices, edible oil

crisil-research.title: IFRS 9 credit and haircut model development for HNI portfolios
crisil-research.description: End-to-end development and delivery of a suite of market risk and credit risk models and Development of haircut, IRB and IFRS 9 PD/ LGD models in compliance with Bank of Thailand guidelines

crisil-research.title: Quickonomics: Cash and kind(le)
crisil-research.description: Given their rising scale and scope, cash transfers by states may be emerging as a new buffer for India’s household consumption

ssga-insights.title: Tactical shift favors USD
ssga-insights.description: Geopolitical risk dominated April FX, with Iran tensions and oil volatility. We turned tactically positive on the USD amid resilient US growth and uncertainty.

ssga-insights.title: What is sector investing?
ssga-insights.description: Discover sector investing—and how it can help investors express their market views and target trends to capture upside potential without putting too much into any one stock.

ssga-insights.title: Monthly Cash Review – USD
ssga-insights.description: April calmed after March, but oil swings kept volatility alive. The Fed stayed on hold and signaled higher-for-longer; we modestly extended to lock in yield.

ssga-insights.title: Risk-on ETF flows in a river of hills market
ssga-insights.description: Markets surged in April amid volatility, with global equities and ETFs seeing strong flows as risk assets climbed despite uncertain headlines.

ssga-insights.title: What is the rising gold/copper price ratio signaling for investors?
ssga-insights.description: Today’s rising gold/copper ratio is less about growth fears—and more about mounting concerns over debt, debasement, and the global monetary order.

ssga-insights.title: What has happened to the great rotation?
ssga-insights.description: Geopolitical strife has led a pause in the “great rotation”, with US stocks rebounding. Profits are robust but leadership remains narrow and risks are elevated.

ssga-insights.title: Central banks hold, but tensions drive risk
ssga-insights.description: Central banks are holding steady, but rising geopolitical risks and stubborn inflation are pushing policymakers closer to renewed rate hikes across major economies.

ssga-insights.title: Raising the wealth management bar for Gen X women
ssga-insights.description: Despite making remarkable economic strides, Gen X women feel vastly underserved. And they present advisors with the opportunity to grow their business while helping to close the gender wealth gap.

ssga-insights.title: Internal succession planning: 5 key steps
ssga-insights.description: Many retiring financial advisors plan to pass the baton to an internal successor. Learn best practices to develop talent and execute a seamless transition.

ssga-insights.title: Iran war: Risk on, energy off
ssga-insights.description: Markets cheered the ceasefire, but the energy shock persists. Stagflation looks unlikely, yet oil shortages are straining economies and driving asset divergence.

ssga-insights.title: Comparing SPY and SPYM: Two State Street SPDR ETFs for S&P 500® exposure
ssga-insights.description: Seeking exposure to the S&P 500® Index? Compare two State Street SPDR ETFs to determine which may be a better fit for your specific investment style and priorities.

ssga-insights.title: Geopolitics at the center of financial markets
ssga-insights.description: Explore how geopolitics drives financial markets and why investors can benefit from recognizing long-term secular shifts that could impact investment decisions.

ssga-insights.title: Turning crisis into engagement: Participant outreach for Defined Contribution plans
ssga-insights.description: In times of crisis, DC plan participants are looking to their employers or plan sponsors for leadership, outreach and guidance.

ssga-insights.title: The transition of power at the Federal Reserve
ssga-insights.description: Fed transition worries look like a tempest in a teapot as institutional resilience holds and geopolitics shape policy.

ssga-insights.title: Nature and biodiversity data: types and uses for investors
ssga-insights.description: A review of nature and biodiversity data types available to public market investors, mapped to portfolio objectives, with an assessment of current limitations.

ssga-insights.title: Private US multifamily real estate looks attractive
ssga-insights.description: US multifamily real estate looks more attractive as supply peaks, homeownership stays costly and pricing resets support long-term opportunity.

ssga-insights.title: Europe services slowdown deepens growth concerns
ssga-insights.description: Global manufacturing momentum contrasts with weakening services as central banks stay cautious, oil prices rise, and geopolitics add downside risks to growth.

ssga-insights.title: Global High Yield Update—Q1 2026
ssga-insights.description: GHY is a carry asset operating in a new regime. Despite likely less-linear returns than previously it remains very functional as a strategic income allocation.

ssga-insights.title: Understanding markets in the new world order: A geopolitical framework for investors
ssga-insights.description: We link geopolitical change to long-term investment outcomes. This matters because geopolitical concerns are likely to remain critical for investors in coming years.

ssga-insights.title: Optimizing trade execution for ETFs of all levels of liquidity
ssga-insights.description: As a liquidity leader, we provide insights to help assess ETF liquidity and evaluate different execution strategies, regardless of liquidity profile.

ssga-insights.title: Why various market risk metrics tell different stories
ssga-insights.description: We explain why investors need to be thoughtful about the volatility metrics they use to understand market reactions.

ssga-insights.title: Three market trends to watch beyond war-driven headlines
ssga-insights.description: Look beyond conflict-driven market moves. Explore three structural trends shaping returns: non-US equities, US corporate re-leveraging, and sticky inflation.

ssga-insights.title: Mega-cap IPOs: Implications for institutional investors and index managers
ssga-insights.description: A potential wave of mega-cap IPOs in 2026, including large venture-backed firms, could reshape global and US equity benchmarks.

ssga-insights.title: Long-Term Asset Class Forecasts: Q2 2026
ssga-insights.description: Our long-term asset class forecasts are forward-looking estimates of total return and risk premia for major asset classes. Read our update for Q2 2026.

ssga-insights.title: Market Forecasts: Q2 2026
ssga-insights.description: War in Iran has upended economic and market expectations. Central bank messaging shifts, surging oil prices, and a threat of escalation has stoked uncertainty.
